---
layout: post
title: "The Key That Shipped in the Browser"
subtitle: "HTB MakeSense, where a theme ships its own encryption key to every browser, a review bot lends you its admin hands, and root learns to read a shell off a picture you painted for it"
date: 2026-07-14 12:00:00 +0000
description: "HTB MakeSense walkthrough. A medium Linux box where a client-side AES key, an unlocked headless-Chrome debug port, and a root OCR service chain to root. Sealed while the box is live."
image: /assets/og/makesense.png
tags: [hackthebox, writeup]
---

MakeSense hands you its own key on the way in. The site encrypts your messages with real crypto and then ships the key to every browser that loads the page, which is the same as shipping no key at all. From there the box becomes a relay of borrowed hands. A bot lends you its admin session, a browser lends you its account, and a service that reads pictures out loud lends you root. The machine talks to no one on the outside, so every answer has to come back through a door it left open by design. You stop expecting it to call, and you start reading the notes it leaves in rooms you can walk into.

```
        m a k e s e n s e            (medium · linux)
        =========================
        the lock is real.    the key is in your pocket.
        you write the note.  a bot signs it as admin.
        you draw a shell.    root reads it out loud.
                        |
                        v
        the wire is cut, so nothing calls home.
        every answer comes back through a door
        the box agreed to leave open.
                                              . . .
```

The full chain, from a forged encrypted blob to a root shell drawn as a picture, is written up below in the usual detail. Because MakeSense is **still active on Hack The Box**, that write-up is sealed. It unseals the moment the box retires. If you already hold the passphrase, the gate is right here.

<script type="application/json" id="lock-data">{"v":1,"iter":250000,"salt":"nqh0Q6Bnc/7WS7/9NlgL2A==","iv":"Ldc42wpdkdQ/oZH9","ct":""}</script>

{% include locked-writeup.html %}

---

*HTB: MakeSense, a medium Linux box that is really a lecture on client-side secrets, the illusion of silence, and every door that answers to one reused key. Still active. The full chain unseals here the day it retires, no live payloads either way.*
